title: PDF Scanner: Replace Live Camera with Choose from Files + Auto Edge Detection & Full Scan-to-PDF Workflow

requirements.text: In the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), remove the 'Live Scanner' camera button entirely. Replace it with only a 'Choose from Files' button (on mobile, label it 'Choose from Files (Google Files / Scanner)'). When clicked, open a native file picker accepting image files (JPG, PNG, JPEG, WEBP) and PDF files. Selected image files are added directly as scanned page entries. Selected PDF files have their pages rendered as individual scanned page entries using PDF.js loaded from CDN. All imported pages must appear in the scanned pages thumbnail list and participate in all subsequent crop, adjustment, and 'Convert to PDF' export operations. Remove all camera/getUserMedia/video-element code from the component.

quotes: removed live camera button and add choose from files button

acceptanceCriteria: No 'Live Scanner' or camera button is visible in the PDF Scanner panel.
A 'Choose from Files' button is prominently displayed; on mobile it reads 'Choose from Files (Google Files / Scanner)'.
Clicking the button opens a native file picker accepting image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp, and application/pdf MIME types.
Selected image files are immediately appended to the scanned pages list as individual entries.
Selected PDF files have each page rendered via PDF.js and appended as individual scanned page entries.
No navigator.mediaDevices or getUserMedia calls remain in the component.
All imported pages appear as numbered thumbnails in the scanned pages list panel.

requirements.text: Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), after a user imports image files via the 'Choose from Files' button, automatically detect document/page edges for each imported image using a Canvas-based edge detection algorithm (pixel contrast analysis or equivalent). Apply an automatic perspective crop to the detected document quadrilateral without requiring manual user interaction. Display the auto-cropped result as the default preview for each scanned page. Provide a 'Manual Crop' fallback mode with draggable corner handles so the user can override the automatic crop if needed. Provide an 'Apply Crop' button to confirm and a 'Reset' button to revert to the uncropped original.

quotes: user capture image and automatic detected page scan and crop

acceptanceCriteria: After a file is imported, the component attempts automatic document edge detection on the Canvas.
A perspective-corrected crop is applied automatically to each imported image and shown as the default preview.
A toggle or radio control allows switching between Auto Crop and Manual Crop modes.
In Manual Crop mode, an interactive overlay with draggable corner handles is shown on the image.
An 'Apply Crop' button confirms the crop and updates the scanned page entry.
A 'Reset' button reverts the page to the uncropped original image.
If auto-detection fails or finds no clear edges, the full image is used as the default without crashing.

requirements.text: Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), provide per-page image adjustment controls for each scanned/imported page: a Brightness slider (range -100 to +100), a Contrast slider (range -100 to +100), and a 'Darken Text' toggle that applies a threshold/levels filter via Canvas to boost dark pixels and lighten the background for improved text legibility. Apply all adjustments in real time on the Canvas preview of the currently selected page. Store adjustments per page so each page can have independent settings.

quotes: allow to adjest brightness and contrast and also allow to dark text

acceptanceCriteria: Each scanned page has its own independent Brightness slider (range -100 to +100).
Each scanned page has its own independent Contrast slider (range -100 to +100).
A 'Darken Text' toggle is present for each page; enabling it applies a Canvas threshold/levels filter that darkens text and lightens the background.
All adjustments update the Canvas preview in real time when sliders or the toggle are changed.
Adjustments are stored per page so switching between pages in the list shows each page's own settings.
Adjustment settings are applied when generating the final PDF output.

requirements.text: Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), ensure the scanned pages list panel displays thumbnails of all imported and processed pages in order. Each thumbnail must show its page number and a remove (✕) button to delete that page from the list. The user must be able to import additional pages at any time using the 'Choose from Files' button, appending them to the existing list.

quotes: after scanning one by one document image converting in pdf

acceptanceCriteria: The scanned pages list panel displays a thumbnail and page number for each imported page.
Each thumbnail has a visible ✕ remove button; clicking it removes that page from the list.
The user can click 'Choose from Files' again at any time to append more pages to the existing list.
The list updates immediately when pages are added or removed.
Page numbers update correctly after removal.

requirements.text: Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), implement a 'Convert to PDF' button that takes all scanned and processed pages (with individual crop and adjustment settings applied) and generates a single PDF document using pdf-lib loaded from CDN (following the same CDN-load pattern used in usePDFOperations.ts). Each page image is embedded as a full-page entry in the output PDF. Show a loading/progress indicator during generation. After generation completes, display a 'Download PDF' button that triggers a browser file download of the resulting PDF.

quotes: converting in pdf and permition save

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Convert to PDF' button is visible when at least one scanned page exists in the list.
Clicking it applies each page's crop and adjustment settings to the Canvas, then embeds the result as a full-page image in the output PDF using pdf-lib.
A loading/progress indicator is shown during PDF generation.
After generation, a 'Download PDF' button appears and triggers a browser file download of the resulting PDF.
The generated PDF contains all non-removed pages in their listed order.
No errors are thrown if a page's canvas or image data is missing; such pages are skipped with a warning.

requirements.text: Within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx), add a filename input field that allows the user to specify a custom filename for the exported PDF before downloading. Pre-fill the input with a default name (e.g., 'scanned-document.pdf'). When the download is triggered, use the specified filename. Validate that the filename is non-empty; if the user omits the '.pdf' extension, append it automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: A filename input field is visible in the export/download area of the PDF Scanner panel.
The input is pre-filled with 'scanned-document.pdf' by default.
The downloaded file uses the user-specified filename.
If the filename is empty, downloading is blocked or the default name is used.
If the user enters a name without '.pdf', the extension is appended automatically before download.

requirements.text: Apply the existing dark-first glassmorphism design system to all updated and new UI elements within the PDF Scanner component (frontend/src/modules/PDFConverter/PDFScanner.tsx). Use glass-card surfaces for the file import panel, scanned pages list, adjustment controls panel, and export controls. Use tool-btn CSS classes for all action buttons ('Choose from Files', 'Apply Crop', 'Reset', 'Convert to PDF', 'Download PDF'). Use teal/cyan accent colors for active states and primary action buttons. Ensure the layout is fully mobile-responsive, stacking the file import area, scanned pages list, and adjustment/export controls vertically on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: All panels in the PDF Scanner use the glass-card CSS class for surfaces.
All action buttons use the tool-btn CSS class.
Teal/cyan accent colors are applied to primary action buttons and active states.
On mobile screens, all panels stack vertically without horizontal overflow.
The design is visually consistent with the other PDF Converter tabs and the rest of the application.

constraints: Do not add or restore any camera (getUserMedia) functionality to the PDF Scanner component.
All processing must remain entirely client-side; no backend changes are required.
pdf-lib and PDF.js must be loaded from CDN following the same pattern used in usePDFOperations.ts.
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Live camera viewfinder or video capture in PDF Scanner.
AI/ML-based document edge detection (Canvas pixel contrast analysis is sufficient).
Changes to any module other than PDFScanner.tsx and its direct dependencies.
